opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't catch my breath no matter what I do, and my chest feels completely heavy and exhausted.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Breathing has been getting rapidly worse over the last 12 hours, a day or so after a choking/aspiration episode, and nothing helps.

symptom_narrative.onset: rapidly worsening over the last 12 hours
symptom_narrative.character: severe breathlessness, chest feels heavy, complete air hunger
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: extreme — exhausted from trying to breathe
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — sitting up hasn't helped at all
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: nothing specific makes it worse, it's just constant
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: dry cough that isn't bringing anything up, feeling very tired and unwell, had a bad choking episode on food about a day and a half ago
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no history of heart failure or needing to sleep sitting up before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely bedbound, too exhausted to move or talk much

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: live-in carer present but unable to manage the level of distress; recently finished antibiotics for the aspiration event

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a consumer pulse oximeter, in a well-ventilated room, during severe respiratory distress — reading is concerning even allowing for device limitations

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: extremely fatigued, trying to conserve energy, may under-report severity due to sluggishness from hypoxaemia

communication_style.typical_message_style: very brief, slow responses; carer relaying most details; mentally sluggish due to hypoxaemia

access_barriers.transport: carer present but not equipped to manage this level of respiratory distress
